learnings.subject: Emergency Dematter Cream
learnings.learning: Dematter paid ROAS fell to RED (1.43 vs 2.07 breakeven) in the same window its 'detangler for dogs' organic rank slid 8->13 - possible pattern: rising paid spend masking organic decline; check organic rank before scaling Dematter paid.
evidence: Organic rank 'detangler for dogs' 8->13 (H10 Daily Keyword Tracker, 2026-07-08)

2026-07-10: 'detangler for dogs' rank slid further to 7 (from a baseline of 5), and 'dog detangler' dropped 9 spots (7->16) - organic instability continues, but paid ROAS remains unverifiable (Supermetrics down 2nd straight day) so this cannot be confirmed or contradicted today
learnings.action_implication: Before any Dematter budget increase, verify organic ranks are stable; if organic is sliding, fix the listing before adding spend.

learnings.subject: Detangling Treatment / 4-in-1 Shampoo / Pin Brush
learnings.learning: Three low-spend SKUs ($3-$12/day) each spent with zero attributed sales on 2026-07-08 - sub-$15 daily budgets may be too small to exit the learning phase and convert; trickle spend produced $19.12 total loss with nothing learned.
evidence: Detangling $11.51 / Shampoo $3.00 / Pin Brush $4.61, all ROAS 0.00 (Supermetrics Query B, 2026-07-08)
learnings.action_implication: For these SKUs: either fund a campaign at a viable test budget with a defined kill threshold, or pause entirely - avoid indefinite trickle spend.

learnings.subject: Account tracking hygiene
learnings.learning: Three ASINs were spending in the ad account without existing in roas_bands.json - one at $166.90/day (2nd largest in account) - untracked spend can run unnoticed until the per-SKU pipeline works; every new campaign launch must add its ASIN to roas_bands.json the same day.
evidence: B0GT1H84JM $166.90 spend ROAS 1.41, unrecognized (Supermetrics Query B, 2026-07-08)
learnings.action_implication: Launch checklist rule for Angelo: campaign live -> ASIN added to roas_bands.json same day. JARVIS flags any unrecognized ASIN as RED automatically.

learnings.subject: Supermetrics schedule_id pipeline
learnings.learning: All 6 Supermetrics schedule_ids submitted by Evening Pre-Run at 2026-07-08 23:04:59 PT had expired by the morning collection attempt (~2026-07-09 07:50 PT, ~8h45m gap) - every query returned 'schedule_id has expired' rather than results. This follows 4 consecutive prior days of partial/missing Supermetrics data already noted on 2026-07-08 - today is the first total (6-for-6) failure. The evening-to-morning gap likely exceeds Supermetrics' result-retention window.
evidence: All 6 schedule_ids (A-F) returned RuntimeError 'schedule_id has expired' on first poll, 2026-07-09 morning run

2026-07-10: same failure repeated - schedule_ids submitted 2026-07-09 23:05:55 PT, all 6 (A-F) again returned 'schedule_id has expired' on first poll at the 2026-07-10 morning run (~8h45m gap again). 2nd consecutive day of total (6-for-6) outage - confirms this is a systemic gap-length issue, not a one-off.
learnings.action_implication: Shorten the gap between Evening Pre-Run submission and Morning collection (e.g. submit closer to sunrise), or confirm the actual result-retention window with Supermetrics support and adjust the 26h staleness check accordingly. Until fixed, treat every morning run as having a real chance of zero ad-data visibility. 2 consecutive total-outage days - escalate to Will for a pipeline fix decision.

learnings.subject: Save the Shave UGC Bill (Meta campaign)
learnings.learning: Meta campaign 'Save the Shave UGC Bill _UGC_070326' breached the $75 cumulative-spend / ROAS<1.0 kill threshold on 2026-07-09 ($130.89 spend, ROAS 0.52) and was flagged for a kill/revise decision. As of 2026-07-10 it has continued spending past the threshold ($154.29 MTD, ROAS 0.44) with no kill/revise decision recorded - the flag alone does not stop spend without a human action.
evidence: 2026-07-10: $154.29 MTD spend, ROAS 0.44 - spend increased $23.40 overnight with no revise/kill applied (Meta MCP, account 2158768751536031)
learnings.action_implication: A kill-threshold flag needs a same-day or next-day human decision (Will) to actually stop spend - repeated flags without follow-through just document the loss rather than prevent it. Consider escalating unresolved kill-threshold breaches after 1 day instead of re-flagging silently.
